name: Bérénice
dev_strap: Terraced hillside residences above the Ionian
sub: A second-floor two-bedroom residence of over 140 square metres gross at the centre of Green Coast.
description: A second-floor two-bedroom residence of over 140 square metres gross at the centre of Green Coast, Palasë. Family scale in the stone village quarter — steps from the central plaza, the pools and the Blue Flag beach that has made this coast Albania's most sought-after address.
highlights: Upper eastern quarter, among garden courtyards
Pools & Blue Flag beach below the resort
location_body: Green Coast is the Albanian Riviera's most established resort address — a stone-built village above the Blue Flag beach at Palasë, where the Ceraunian mountains meet the Ionian. Mediterranean architecture in local stone, travertine and timber; promenades, pools, restaurants and a beach club below; the life of the coast a few steps from the door.
position_copy: Bérénice sits in the upper eastern quarter of the village, set among garden courtyards above the central plaza. The hotel is a short walk north-west, the parking entrance across the village, and the paths down to the beach begin at the village edge.
